verdicts.claim: extreme market-wide breadth + alert coin dumping -> bounce (long)
verdicts.ruling: TRADEABLE (modest, val-confirmed). Best horizon ~4h (f240). 1-day horizon fails.
verdicts.spec: breadth_min>=16 AND chg<=-2.5 ; side=LONG ; hold ~4h (f240). Entry = alert coin's entry_px.

control_breadth_isolation.interpretation: A -2.5% dump alone bleeds (low-breadth -0.65%); the SAME dump during a market-wide flush bounces (+1.60%). Breadth adds real info.

honesty_caveats: Per-TRADE val mean +1.26% (p<1e-4) is inflated by within-day clustering: extreme-breadth minutes fire dozens of correlated alerts. Independent events ≈ 10 val days.
Per-DAY val mean +0.15% (p=0.77) NOT significant — treat as 'few independent events, positive 7/10 val days, modest per-event edge', not 753 iid trades.
f1440 (24h) FAILS val (-0.15%): bounce is a 1-4h phenomenon, fades by next day.
dev maxday=0.91 (10-10) — would auto-fail the single-day rule on dev alone; rescued ONLY because val (no 10-10) independently confirms + control test isolates breadth.

verdicts.claim: xwin=1 / n1h-n24h high / minsprev small = '真信号'值得做
verdicts.ruling: FALSE — classic left-tail trap. Positive median but NEGATIVE short mean (rare squeezes blow out the short). Not tradeable either side after cost.
verdicts.note: e.g. xwin=1 short: median +0.76% but mean -1.00%, win 53%. f240 median climbs to +2.7% while mean stays -1.2% = a few violent squeezes dominate. As LONG these have negative median. f5 immediate-dump shorts: gross only +0.08-0.15%, fully eaten by ~0.75% RT cost.

verdicts.ruling: No additional tradeable edge. hb_strongest-mover long: mean +0.57% but median NEGATIVE + dropTop<0 (mirror trap). first_day/n1h=1 long ~flat. breadth_1h high subsumed by breadth_min signal.

bottom_line.candidate: extreme_breadth_dipbuy (breadth_min>=16 & chg<=-2.5, LONG, ~4h hold): val +1.26% net/trade, win 72%, 7/10 val days positive — but few independent events (~10/2wk) and day-level not significant. Size accordingly.
debunked: xwin/repeat/dense-spacing = left-tail squeeze traps, not alpha
bottom_line.family_honest_summary: One genuine but small/low-frequency edge (panic-flush dip-buy). The rest of the sequence/cluster/breadth family is either single-day artifact or left-tail trap. No high-frequency tradeable alpha found.
